Instruction: You are a nutritionist (User ID: user_404). Ask the AI Service Agent to check the flavor characteristics of the first box of purple cookies from the left on the third shelf. Next, ask the AI Service Agent to check whether the *** hazelnut cookies to the right of the dark brown rectangular cookies with a yellow lemon pattern on the third shelf have the "nutty" characteristic. If yes and their sugar content exceeds 50 grams, ask the AI Service Agent to add one unit of that hazelnut cookie to your cart. If not, ask the AI Service Agent to add one unit of the purple box to your cart (provided it has a discount factor better than 0.9 and a price no higher than 30). Afterwards, ask the AI Service Agent to check your shopping list, add any products on the list that are not yet in the cart to the cart, and ask the AI Service Agent to calculate the total carbohydrates of all items in the cart.
analysis:
1. Check whether the first box of purple cookies from the left on the third shelf and the hazelnut cookies located to the right of the dark brown rectangular cookies have the "nutty" feature.

2. If they possess this feature and the sugar content exceeds 50 grams, add 1 unit of the hazelnut cookies to the cart.

3. If not, add 1 unit of the purple cookies, provided the discount factor is less than 0.8.

4. Check the shopping list and add any items that have not yet been placed in the shopping cart.

5. Calculate the total carbohydrate content.

Instruction: You are a food blogger (User ID: user_505). Ask the AI Service Agent to check the allergen ingredients of the orange cookies on the second shelf that you are pointing at first. Next, ask the AI Service Agent to check whether the dark brown rectangular cookies with a yellow lemon pattern on the third shelf contain fewer types of allergens than the orange box. If they have fewer allergens and are from the UK, ask the AI Service Agent to add one unit of the dark brown box to your cart. If not, ask the AI Service Agent to search for cookies in the current product database that are labeled "High Fat" and contain gluten allergens, and add the cheapest one to your cart. If there are multiple cookies tied for the lowest price among those that meet the conditions, you want all of them to be added. Finally, ask the AI Service Agent to check your cart to ensure there are no duplicates and calculate the total amount payable.
analysis:
1. Check the allergens of the first orange cookie you point to on the second layer, and compare them with the allergen types of the dark brown rectangular cookies on the third layer to determine if the dark brown cookies have fewer allergen types.

2. If they have fewer allergen types and are produced in the UK, add one piece of the dark brown cookie to your cart.

3. If not, search for cookies tagged with "High Fat" and containing gluten, and add the cheapest qualifying cookie to the cart. If there are multiple cookies tied for the lowest price among those that meet the conditions, add one of each of those tied items.

4. Check the shopping cart to ensure there are no duplicate items.

5. Calculate the total amount payable.

Results:
You are a nutritionist (User ID: user_606). Ask the AI Service Agent to check the sugar content of the blue cookies located in the middle directly below the first pointed-at product, then have the AI Service Agent check whether the sugar content of the dark brown rectangular cookies with a yellow lemon pattern on the third shelf is lower than that of the blue package. If it is lower and does not contain the "soy" allergen, ask the AI Service Agent to add two items of the dark brown package to your cart. If not, ask the AI Service Agent to search the current product database for items similar in flavor to the blue package and currently on discount, and add one of the lowest-calorie options to the cart. If multiple items tie for the lowest calorie count, add one of each tied item. Afterwards, ask the AI Service Agent to check your shopping list to confirm that the item has not yet been included in the purchase plan, and have the AI Service Agent calculate the total tax for the items in the cart.
analysis:
1. Check the sugar content of the blue cookie directly below the first pointer, and verify whether the sugar content of the dark brown rectangular cookie on the third layer is lower than that of the blue cookie.
2. If it is lower and does not contain "soy", add 2 pieces of the dark brown cookie to the cart.
3. If not, search for discounted items with a flavor similar to the blue cookie, and add 1 piece of the item with the lowest calories. If multiple items tie for the lowest calorie count, add 1 piece of each of those tied items.
4. Check the shopping list to ensure there are no duplicate items.
5. Calculate the total tax.
